title: PARENT CHILD INTERACTIVE THERAPY - 211info
text: JACKSON COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH PARENT CHILD INTERACTIVE THERAPY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides short-term, specialized behavior management in order to assist in managing difficult behaviors including, but not limited to, tantrums, separation anxiety, developmental concerns (such as autism or language problems), histories of trauma, property destruction, and parent-child relationship problems. The program requires weekly appointments and typically lasts 18-24 weeks. 140 S Holly Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Ages 1-10 who display challenging behaviors and their parents (biological, foster/resource, or adoptive), grandparents, legal guardians, or kinship caregivers Hours: Phone hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Classes: No standard hours Email (541) 774-3333 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 774-8201 Crisis Line (541) 774-3333 Scheduling and Business Line Main phone (541) 774-3333 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call to enroll or visit website for more information FEES: Varies by service. Accepts OHP OpenCard, Medicare, and most private insurances. Sliding scale fees available for uninsured patients. No one is turned away for inability to pay. DOCUMENTS: Insurance information and guardianship documentation LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Jackson County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Between Holly and Ivy Streets off W 8th Avenue Services: Parent Child Interactive Therapy Other Services Offered At This Location: MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - JACKSON COUNTY CRISIS LINE MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES MENTAL HEALTH SUPPORT SERVICES - EARLY ASSESSMENT AND SUPPORT ALLIANCE REHABILITATION/HABILITATION SERVICES - DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES SERVICES SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - SYRINGE EXCHANGE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. JACKSON COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH
